calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion On Petition For Order To Show Cause And Order Compelling Compliance With Investigatory Subpoena
Off Calendar Mar-08-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. GREG W. CHAMBERS, Esq. of California Attorney General's Office (Tel: 415-510-3382) for Petitioner Medical Board of California;
Atty. KEVIN C. MURPHY, Esq. of Murphy Jones APC (Tel: 619-684-5073) for Respondent Thomas A. Gonda, Jr., MFT, M.D.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA's Motion On Petition For Order To Show Cause And Order Compelling Compliance With Investigatory Subpoena.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for parties (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion is denied: The petition may be filed in (1) "the county in which any hearing is held"; (2) "the county in which ... any investigation is conducted under the direction of the head of a department"; and (3) "the county in which testimony is designated to be given or documents or other items are designated to be produced." Given those conditions, there is no evidence submitted showing that San Francisco is the proper venue.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the pro tem judge at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
